title: Split Behavior in Tool-Using LLM Agents
thumbnail_alt: Diagram of an LLM agent producing refusal text while still making a prohibited tool call.
description: Studies cases where models produce refusal-like text while still executing prohibited or harmful tool actions.

title: David vs. Goliath: Verifiable Agent-to-Agent Jailbreaking via Reinforcement Learning
thumbnail_alt: Schematic of an attacker, victim agent, verifier, and tool-call environment.
description: Introduces Tag-Along Attacks and Slingshot, an RL framework for discovering verifiable jailbreaks against tool-using LLM agents.

title: Punctuation and Predicates in Language Models
thumbnail_alt: Layer-wise heatmap-style thumbnail for punctuation and predicate processing in language models.
description: Mechanistic interpretability work on how punctuation and predicates are processed and propagated across layers in language models.

title: Controllability, not predictability, flexibly gates the impact of Pavlovian bias on action selection
thumbnail_alt: Abstract thumbnail showing controllability gating action selection.
description: Computational neuroscience work on how perceived controllability changes the influence of Pavlovian bias on decision-making.
